fields.description: This is a variation of classic apple pie, but instead of a top crust, we’re piling on a thick, buttery brown sugar crumble. This apple crumble pie is the definition of fall comfort, and you don’t need to fuss with a decorative pie crust on top. Make sure to chill each component as directed. Cold pie dough and a cold crumble topping hold their shape much better during baking.
ingredients: 1 and 1/2 cups (188g) all-purpose flour (spooned & leveled), plus more for work surface
1/2 cup (8 Tbsp; 113g) cold unsalted butter, cubed
1/4 cup (60g/ml) ice-cold water, plus more as needed
egg wash: 1 large egg beaten with 1 Tablespoon (15g/ml) milk
1/2 cup (100g) packed light or dark brown sugar
5 Tablespoons (71g) unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled
10 cups (1.25kg) 1/4-inch-thick apple slices (about 8 large peeled and cored apples)*
fields.title: Apple Crumble Pie

3 medium apples, peeled and diced into small bite-size pieces (about 2 and 1/2 cups, or 300g, diced)

optional: homemade salted caramel for topping

1 1/2 cups sliced Turkish or English cucumber

fields.description: These Falafel Bowls are made with your choice of grain, lots of healthy veggies and drizzled with tahini sauce- the falafels can be prepped ahead for the busy workweek. Vegan and Gluten-free! USE dry chickpeas, soaked in water for 8-12 hours or overnight. DO NOT use canned chickpeas.
ingredients: 3/4 cup dry chickpeas (yields 2 cups soaked), soaked in water until plump- 8-24 hours  (do not use cooked or canned chickpeas-please trust me!)
1 cup cilantro (stems ok) or sub-Italian parsley

1/2 a jalapeno, seeds ok (add the entire thing if you want a little kick)
1/2 tsp kosher salt (add more to taste, I use 3/4 teaspoon)
3 cups cooked grain: basmati rice, quinoa, farro, Freekeh, etc.
2 cups diced tomatoes, or cherry tomatoes
4 cups greens – baby arugula, baby spinach or kale, etc…

other optional additions: avocado, grated carrot, grated beets, or sliced radishes, micro greens or fresh herbs like cilantro or flat-leaf parsley, Pickled Onions or Pickled Turnips  (something pickled or fermented is nice, even kraut)

nutrition.servingSize: 3 falafels with quinoa, veggies and tahini sauce

fields.description: It'll be a sad day in Paris if Chartier ever shuts its doors. True, the food isn't exceptional. But it's cheap and people seem to flock here in droves. And the interior? I don't think you'll find a more perfectly-preserved relic of an old Paris, with glass-globe fixtures, tables jammed together, coat racks high above the tables, and a menu that hasn't made a single

1 large fennel bulb, cored and thinly sliced ( or sub celery and more asparagus)
1 large leek, white and light green parts, thinly sliced into half moons

fields.description: These make-ahead Savory Crepes can be assembled the day before, then warmed up in a baking dish just as your guests arrive. Deliciously savory and hearty, they will satisfy and delight. Use this recipe as a jumping-off point for your own delicious fillings.
ingredients: 1 cup flour – or use gluten-free flour ( like Bob’s Red Mill)

2 medium leeks, sliced into 1/8-inch rings (white and light green parts only). If leeks are very dirty, place rings in a bowl of cool water to loosen dirt, swish with your hand, strain, and repeat if necessary.
1 pound mushrooms, sliced (button, cremini, shiitake, morels)
1 bunch asparagus, tough ends removed, chopped into 1-inch pieces
1 tablespoon chopped fresh tarragon (optional, but delicious)
6 ounces goat cheese ( or sub cream cheese)

For
ingredients: 6 tablespoons (3 ounces, 90g) unsalted butter (chilled and cubed)

fields.description: A classic lattice-topped homemade blueberry peach pie bursting with juicy flavor. Serve with some vanilla ice cream for an unforgettable summer treat.

But if you want to try them with dark chocolate, I’m sure they would be fine. Speaking of milk chocolate, be sure to use good-quality dark milk chocolate. The bars you buy at the supermarket check-out stands are us

fields.description: How to Make Beet Kvass! An Eastern European probiotic drink made with beets, sea salt and water. Full of healthy probiotics from the Lacto-fermentation, Beet Kvass is believed to help boost immunity.  It tastes slightly sweet, tangy, earthy and salty- but in a good way! With just 10 minutes of hands-on time,it is so simple- just let mother nature take its course.  (Allow 2 weeks for fermentation- see notes for speeding up this process.)
ingredients: 2 medium beets, organic (2 cups diced, with skin on)
3 cups filtered water (tap water may have too much chlorine & inhibit fermentation)

¼ cup whey (the liquid from strained yogurt) or fermented pickle brine or fermented sauerkraut brine. (Make doubly sure the brine is fermented, made with salt only, no vinegar!). You can also use ¼ cup of a previous batch of kvass, or store-bought kvass.

fields.description: Look no further for a creamy and ultra smooth classic cheesecake recipe! Paired with a buttery graham cracker crust, no one can deny its simple decadence. For the best results, bake in a water bath. This recipe is also in my cookbook, Sally's Baking 101.
ingredients: 1 and 1/2 cups (180g) graham cracker crumbs (about 12 full-sheet graham crackers)
32 ounces (904g) full-fat brick cream cheese, softened to room temperature

fields.description: Two favorite indulgences come together to make one epic dessert in this cinnamon-roll inspired cheesecake. Swirl crumbles of buttery cinnamon-brown sugar filling into layers of rich, creamy cheesecake, and bake atop a thick graham cracker crust. Top with brown sugar whipped cream and a sprinkle of cinnamon for the perfect finishing touch.
ingredients: 1 and 1/2 cups (180g) graham cracker crumbs (about 12 full-sheet graham crackers)
32 ounces (904g) full-fat brick cream cheese, softened to room temperature
3/4 cup (180g) full-fat sour cream, at room temperature

3/4 cup (150g) packed light or dark brown sugar
2 Tablespoons light or dark brown sugar

Second, let the batter cool for a few minutes before adding the eggs so you don’t ‘cook’ them. When you stir in the eggs that you do it vigorously, and without stopping. I’m not a fan of extra dishes to wash, but a stand mixer is the best w

fields.description: A simple delicious recipe for Creamy Cauliflower Soup with Garlic and Sage  & Toasted Pine Nut Crumble. Vegan adaptable.
ingredients: 2 leeks (about 2 cups) diced,  or sub one large onion, diced
1 head cauliflower- cut into small ½-¾ inch thick pieces (7-8 cups)
4 cups veggie stock ( or chicken stock)
2 Tablespoons fresh sage (or substitute 1 tsp dry herbs de provence)
¼ teaspoon white pepper (optional, but delicious)
½-1 cup sour cream, plain yogurt (or cashew cream) more to taste. Feel free to sub vegan sour cream.

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home Blog
fields.description: This creamy mushroom polenta is so warm and comforting! A simple vegetarian dinner that can be made in under 30 minutes. Cozy and delicious, perfect for busy weeknights.
ingredients: 1 cup polenta (fine ground cornmeal or medium ground cornmeal)
2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil or butter
Optional: 1/4 – 1/2 cup pecorino cheese, parmesan cheese, creamy goat cheese, or any other melty cheese like cheddar, pepper jack, mozzarella or provolone. Add more to taste.
Optional garnish: fresh herbs- basil, Italian parsley, thyme.
3 cups mushrooms, cleaned -chanterelles, morel mushrooms, button mushrooms, cremini, miatake, shitake or portobellos
1-2 tablespoons oil or butter (a mix is nice)
2-3 tablespoons wine- totally optional (cooking sherry is nice- sherry wine, not sherry vinegar!)
1 tablespoon fresh sage, chopped (or sub 1 teaspoon dried sage, rosemary or thyme)
Garnish- a drizzle of truffle oil ( optional- but worth it! )

nutrition.servingSize: 1 bowl (1 cup polenta, ½ cup mushrooms)

fields.title: Mushroom Polenta Recipe

fields.description: These Peanut Butter Blossoms have a soft and chewy peanut butter cookie base with a Hershey's kiss placed in the center. An easy holiday staple!

fields.description: This scaled down recipe yields just 1 giant sugar cookie!
ingredients: 2 Tablespoons (28g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
2 Tablespoons (30ml) beaten egg (crack the egg, beat it, and use 2 tbsp)

fields.author: David
fields.description: A favorite quick-bite on the streets of Paris, at L'As du Fallafel. L'As du Fallafel is one of the few places where Parisians chow down on the street. Beginning with a fork, dig into warm pita bread stuffed with marinated crunchy cabbage, silky eggplant, sesame hoummous, and boules of chick-pea paste, crisp-fried fallafel. Spice it up with a dab of searingly-hot sauce piquante. L'As du
fields.title: L’As du Fallafel

fields.description: A lot of people will be celebrating tonight the release of Beaujolais Nouveau... ...and coincidentally, a lot of people will be waking up with headaches tomorrow. Myself included. Complimentary dégustations through Saturday at: Aux Caves d'Aligre 3, place d'Aligre 12th Tél: 01 43 43 34 26 Related Posts and Places in the Vicinity Blé Sucré: The Best Madeleines in Paris Two Delicious Dining Guides to

fields.description: Where do you go on a rainy afternoon if you find yourself near the middle of Paris? Quite a bit of the town has emptied out, as people make their exodus away from the city for the holidays. Those of us here are celebrating at home - or from the looks of things out there - doing a little last-minute scrambling for holiday gifts. There

fields.description: Walnuts are a type of hickory grown in temperate climates, like California.   They are the second ranking nut crop in the United States, after almonds.  There are two types of walnuts; Black or American, and English or Persian walnut.

1/2 cup (120 grams) sour cream or full fat plain yogurt, (at room temperature)

2 medium bananas the riper the better

fields.description: These soft gingerbread blossoms feature the nostalgic flavors of warm spice and cozy molasses, with a melty chocolate kiss—or white chocolate hug—nestled in the center. This no-chill dough comes together quickly and bakes into perfectly spiced holiday cookies that disappear quickly!

3/4 cup (150g) packed light or dark brown sugar (I use dark)

fields.description: If you want to live in France, you need to get used to people speaking their mind. Years ago when I was young and supple, I'd eat whatever I could get my hands on. And working in a restaurant, well, let's just say that's not the best food to eat on a long-term basis. But I know all-too well about that because I was one

fields.description: Make a batch of these pancakes for a delicious low-cal breakfast. With wholemeal flour to make them more filling, they're topped with an easy blueberry sauce

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: Ultra-soft gingerbread cinnamon rolls with a molasses-spiced brown sugar filling and cinnamon cream cheese frosting. A cozy holiday breakfast or brunch recipe that can be made ahead, refrigerated overnight, or frozen for later. If you're a yeast bread beginner, see my Baking With Yeast guide for extra guidance and success tips.
ingredients: 1 cup (240ml/g) whole milk, warmed to about 100°F (38°C)
6 Tablespoons (75g) packed light or dark brown sugar, divided
1 Tablespoon (9g) active dry or instant yeast
5 Tablespoons (72g) unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled
4 cups (520g) bread flour (spooned & leveled), plus more as needed for hands/work surface
5 Tablespoons (72g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
2 Tablespoons (43g) unsulphured or dark molasses (do not use blackstrap; I prefer Grandma’s brand)
1/2 cup (100g) packed light or dark brown sugar
4 ounces (113g) full-fat block cream cheese, softened to room temperature
2 Tablespoons (30g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature

fields.author: Sally McKenney
fields.description: I’m so grateful that my recipes and website have brought (even a sliver of!) joy to many individuals and families this year. As a sign of my gratitude (and to keep the baking momentum going), I'm offering you the chance to win a brand new KitchenAid stand mixer plus an entire stack of my favorite baking cookbooks, including my 3 published cookbooks as well. There will be 2 winners and you can comment on the blog post to enter. See rules in the blog post. Open to the whole world!

fields.author: David
fields.description: I realized that a little while back I posted some pictures about my visit to Les Crayères, a Michelin-starred restaurant in the Champagne region, about an hour from Paris. But I never wrote about the meal or my experience. Since I've been planning another trip back—hopefully soon, it prompted me to share my lunch, at last. Perhaps some people coming to Paris want to take
fields.title: Les Crayeres

fields.description: Fresh peaches, peach preserves, crisp bacon and ooey gooey melted brie sandwiches made right in the waffle maker! SO EASY!

fields.description: A simplified Indian classic, this Chicken Korma recipe is easy and lively with so much flavor! Tender chicken cooked in a luscious yogurt sauce with fragrant Indian spices.
ingredients: 1 pound chicken breasts (or chicken thighs- *see notes)
1 tablespoon coconut oil (or avocado oil, or other neutral oil)
1/2 cup plain yogurt (full fat was used for this recipe)
1/4 cup raw cashews (or blanched almonds or coconut cream or coconut milk)
1/2 cup chicken broth or vegetable broth
1 teaspoon salt, plus more for seasoning the chicken
1 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder or red chili powder, paprika or 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper
serve with fresh spinach and fresh cilantro

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: These cranberry orange scones are buttery and moist with crisp crumbly edges and soft flaky centers. Crunchy coarse sugar and an orange glaze are the perfect finishing touches! Read through the recipe before beginning. You can skip the chilling for 15 minutes prior to baking, but I highly recommend it to prevent the scones from over-spreading.
ingredients: 2 cups (250g) all-purpose flour (spooned & leveled), plus more for hands and work surface
optional: 1 Tablespoon (15ml) heavy cream and coarse sugar

fields.author: David
fields.description: My perfect day in Paris is one that starts at the Marché d'Aligre. I'd get there first thing in the morning, around 9 A.M. as the flea market vendors are unloading their trucks, scoping out treasures as they unpack them. (Before the rest of humanity descends on the market.) I'd rifle through the boxes of knives, cast-off kitchenware, and perhaps score a vintage Le Creuset
fields.title: Les Provinces and Cafe des Abattoirs

fields.description: Choux pastry is piped and fried to golden perfection in this delicious homemade churro recipe. Serve as is or with warm melted chocolate for dipping!

fields.description: This tea-time favourite is moist, sweet, nutty and comforting - it's bound to be a hit with friends and family
ingredients: 175g softened butter plus extra for greasing

fields.author: David
fields.description: As many readers know, I often did posts which included links to blogs and other places around the web that I found interesting and worth sharing. I now share those links on my Facebook page since I have just too many things to share here on the blog—like all-important stories about Paris bakeries, Italian gents with fabulous hair who scoop gelato, and my ill-fated search
fields.title: Links & Facebook

fields.author: Tessa Arias
fields.description: Moist and tender Double Chocolate Muffins feature a brown sugar cocoa muffin studded with gooey chocolate chunks for the ultimate easy treat! Ready in just 35 minutes, no mixer required.

fields.description: Crowd-pleasing Easter Cupcakes feature a moist, rich chocolate cupcake, topped with coconut buttercream frosting, and garnished with toasted coconut and Easter egg candies. Can be made ahead of time!
ingredients: 1/4 cup (50 grams) packed light brown sugar
1/2 cup full-fat canned coconut milk, (shaken well or whisked until somewhat smooth)
2  large eggs plus one egg yolk, (at room temperature)
8 ounces (227 grams) unsalted butter, (at room temperature)

fields.description: Flourless Chocolate Cake is the perfect recipe for any chocolate lover! A surprisingly easy well-loved recipe for restaurant-quality results.

Freshly whipped cream, vanilla ice cream, or 1:1 ganache

1 tablespoon kosher salt, (plus coarse sea salt for sprinkling)

6 garlic cloves, crushed or finely minced
1 teaspoons black peppercorns or 1/2 teaspoon coarse ground pepper.

ingredients: 1.8g floury baking potatoes such as King Edward or Maris Piper, unpeeled
140g mature or extra mature cheddar grated
600ml beef stock (or chicken for a lighter gravy)

fields.description: This homemade French Silk Pie has an Oreo crust and ultra-creamy chocolate filling with no raw eggs! A perfect make-ahead dessert. My most-popular holiday recipe!
ingredients: 20  Oreo (230 grams) cookies ((regular-stuffed, keep filling intact))
10 tablespoons (142 grams) unsalted butter,  (at room temperature)
Chocolate shavings or cocoa powder, (for garnish if desired)

ingredients: 100g unsalted butter melted, plus extra for the tin

fields.author: David
fields.description: Where does the time go? When people used to say that, I thought they were being overly dramatic. Or worse, meant that I was getting older at a faster clip than I thought. But what I think it means, for all of us, is that life used to roll along at a more leisurely clip, but nowadays, I wake up and find another year has passed.
fields.title: Liza Restaurant, Lebanese Food in Paris

fields.description:
This dish is, of course, all – or mostly – about the cheese. A good cheddar is wonderful, but it’s nice to mix up a sharp cheese with another that’s nutty and slightly creamy, such as Jarlsberg, Comté or Gruyère, in whatever proportion you like.

I cook the sauce in the same pan that I made the pasta in to save on clean up. I seasoned this with dry mustard powder and thyme. The mustard gives a bit of depth to the sauce and the fresh thyme adds a pleasant herbal note in the background. But you
ingredients: 1/4 teaspoon cayenne or red pepper powder
fields.title: Macaroni and Cheese

fields.author: Tonia | Feasting at Home
fields.description: Homemade Chicken Noodle Soup is easy to make and the ultimate comfort food full of immune boosting properties. Healthy, satisfying and comes together in less than an hour!
ingredients: 1 leek, cleaned and sliced in half moons
2 teaspoons dried thyme (or 2 tablespoons of fresh thyme)
2 teaspoons salt (this will depend greatly on the broth you use-adjust to taste)
2-3 tablespoons lemon juice or acv more to taste
fields.title: Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe

fields.author: Tonia | Feasting at Home
fields.description: Chicken Provençal is all about ease and elegance!  Melty tender chicken seasoned with aromatic Herbs de Provence braised in white wine with olives, garlic and shallots.  So simple and so delicious.  Easy enough for a weeknight and elegant enough for company.
ingredients: 2 1/2 pounds chicken thighs and legs, bone-in, skin-on
2 tablespoons Herbs de Provence (store-bought or make your own)
10 large garlic cloves, peeled and left whole
1 1/2 cups fresh tomatoes (grape tomatoes, small Romas or cherry tomatoes are nice left whole)
1 cup pitted olives, whole ( kalamata or green olives)
5 shallots, whole or cut in halves
1/2 cup white wine (sauvignon blanc is nice)
1/2 teaspoon sea salt, adjust to taste
fields.title: Chicken Provençal

fields.author: Chungah
fields.description: Who would have thought? You can bake your eggs right in avocado halves for a healthy breakfast option to start your day off right!
ingredients: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper (to taste)

fields.author: Chungah
fields.description: The most unbelievably easiest quesadillas ever with just 3 stinking ingredients. That’s it! And they’re so tiny and cute!

fields.author: Chungah
fields.description: A classic American diner sandwich, with melted cheesy goodness, a juicy patty and caramelized onions – made in a waffle maker!!!
ingredients: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper (to taste)

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: Brimming with buttery flavor, sweet crunch, and from-scratch caramel, each bite of this homemade caramel corn is undeniably irresistible. You'll make the 6-ingredient caramel sauce on the stove, and while a candy thermometer is helpful, you certainly can get by without one. The recipe below is for plain caramel popcorn, but see Notes for other variations. This is a great make-ahead recipe for a party, bake sale, or gathering, and can easily be doubled.
fields.title: Homemade Caramel Corn

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: Irish Soda Bread is a quick bread that does not require any yeast. Instead, all of its leavening comes from baking soda and buttermilk. This Irish Soda Bread recipe is my grandmother's and has been cherished in my family for years. It's dense, yet soft and has the most incredible crusty exterior.
ingredients: 4 and 1/4 cups (531g) all-purpose flour (spooned & leveled), plus more for your hands and counter
5 Tablespoons (71g) unsalted butter, cold and cubed*

fields.description: An easy family favourite with buttery pastry and sweet dulce de leche. We recommend a generous dollop of cream to serve

fields.author: David
fields.description: A great bakery in Paris making fresh baguettes, croissants and excellent French pastries.

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: These easy healthy berry streusel bars combine two layers of almond oat streusel and one thick layer of berry filling. These dessert bars are vegan. If using certified gluten-free oats, they're gluten free as well!
ingredients: 2 cups (about 300g) fresh or frozen mixed berries (do not thaw & see note)
1 Tablespoon (11g) coconut sugar, brown sugar, or granulated sugar
2 and 1/2 cups (213g) old-fashioned whole rolled oats or quick oats
1/4 cup (56g) coconut oil, melted (or use melted butter)
for topping: 1/2 cup (64g) sliced or chopped almonds and 1 extra Tablespoon oats

fields.author: Sally McKenney
fields.description: Today I hope you'll join me in celebrating 10 years since I first began sharing recipes with the world. In December 2011, I started a food blog that unbeknownst to me would someday host 1,200 recipes used by millions of bakers all over the world. I've spent the past decade developing hundreds of recipes, authoring cookbooks, photographing thousands of photos, building a business, and becoming a boss. And without YOU + my supportive friends, family, and team + all of the blogging friends I've met
fields.title: Happy 10 Years!

fields.author: Suwanee | Feasting at Home
fields.description: This recipe for Chicken Satay is bursting with authentic Thai flavor! Chicken (or tofu) is bathed in a fragrant marinade of coconut milk and Thai spices, then grilled to perfection and served with a flavorful peanut dipping sauce.  Vegan-adaptable! Allow 30 minutes of marinating time.
ingredients: 1 pound chicken, (thighs or breast) sliced thin and long 1/4 inch thick x 3-4 inches long. See notes for tofu.
1 tablespoon yellow curry powder  or sub 1 teaspoon each- turmeric, cumin, and coriander plus 1/4 teaspoon ground ginger
2 tablespoons brown sugar, palm sugar,  agave, maple or honey
2 teaspoons soy sauce, (or try Thai mushroom soy sauce)
2 tablespoons peanut, avocado or olive oil
Garnishes: lime wedges, crushed roasted peanuts, cilantro
1/3 cup peanut butter, smooth or chunky
1 1/2 tablespoons brown sugar, palm sugar, or honey

fields.description: Light the coals and cook up a feast of lobster served with seaweed butter and barbecued T-bone steak. Does anything say summer better than surf & turf?
ingredients: 1 lobster cut lengthways, large claws twisted off and cracked
20g fresh seaweed (or 2 tsp kombu flakes or blitzed nori)

fields.description: This easy apple crisp recipe is bursting with fall flavor. Between the spiced apple filling and buttery oat crumble topping, this classic fall dessert is a must-make treat!

tags: how to make chicken broth from bones
how to make chicken stock from bones

fields.author: David
fields.description: People have been making ice cream far longer than the invention of electricity so there's no reason you can't make ice cream and sorbets at home without a machine! The advantage to using an electric or hand-cranked machine is that the final result will be smoother and creamier. Freezing anything from liquid-to-solid means you're creating hard ice crystals, so if you're making it by hand,
fields.title: How To Make Ice Cream Without a Machine

fields.author: Michelle Lettrich
fields.description: Sugar cookies decorated for a baby shower - onesies, rocking horses, baby carriages and baby chicks.

fields.description: Elegant yet simple, Braised Chicken with Fresh Figs requires only 25 mins of hands-on time before baking in the oven. Perfect for entertaining.

3 medium shallots, sliced (or 1 red onion, sliced)

1/4 cup sherry cooking wine, ruby port, or white wine. ( Or sub 2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar plus 1 tablespoon honey)

fields.description: It's easy to make your own cheese at home. All you need is a bottle of milk, a scoop of yogurt, a touch of vinegar, and a few minutes over the heat. Don't believe me? Check out my guest post at Simply Recipes, a how-to on making Homemade Ricotta Cheese. Related Recipes: Dulce de Leche Cheesecake Brownies Goat Cheese Custard with Strawberries in Red Wine

fields.description: Recipe adapted from The Perfect Scoop Because the flavor (and price) of these peppercorns is so special, I chose not to infuse them in the milk mixture, instead adding them at the end. I left them relatively coarse, since I think it’s interesting to get little “bites” of pepper, although these peppercorns are on the soft side. If using another kind of pepper, use your best judgment, depending on your personal tastes. And, of course, feel free to use more or less than I did.
ingredients: 8 ounces (230g) dark milk chocolate ((at least 35% cacao solids), broken or chopped )
1 1/2 cups (375ml) creme fraiche or heavy cream (or a combination (see Note))
1 tablespoon good-quality ground black peppercorns (I used Tazmanian peppercorns)

fields.description: The first time I ever had gelato was about 20 years ago when it seemed to be all the rage in the 80's, that staggeringly over-the-top era of excess, when we all seemed to be fascinated by Studio 54, record-shopping at the Gap (anyone else remember that?), Bianca Jagger, ultra-suede, and Bill Blass-designed Lincoln Continentals. There was place around the corner from Chez Panisse in

fields.description: These Mediterranean Chickpea Bowls are loaded with crispy roasted chickpeas, quinoa, fresh veggies, and a creamy tahini sauce. A vibrant, high-protein vegan meal that’s perfect for lunch, dinner, or meal prep.
ingredients: 1/2 cup tahini paste (make sure it is stirred)
1/4 –1/3 cup water, more to desired consistency
1–2 tablespoons olive oil (optional, feel free to leave it out if you prefer oil-free)
2 fresh garlic cloves (finely minced – grate it or use a garlic press)
Optional: add 1-2 teaspoons of Zaatar or 1/2 teaspoon each of cumin and coriander)
1 cup cucumber, slice into bite sized pieces
1 cup grape or cherry tomatoes, sliced
1/2 cup red onions, thinly sliced (see notes)
1/2 cup castelvetrano olives or kalamata olives
1/2 cup fresh herbs, chopped (a mix is nice-basil, dill, parsley, oregano)
Sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds and micro greens, if desired

3 to 4  drops green gel food coloring

1/2 teaspoon black pepper, or to taste
2 teaspoons lemon juice or apple cider vinegar, more to taste
Optional: Garnish each bowl with fresh parsley, red pepper flakes, and a drizzle of coconut cream or a dollop of coconut yogurt.

fields.description: Make fresh bagels right at home with this tested dough recipe. Don't skip the water bath and egg wash—both provide an extra chewy and golden brown crust. This recipe is also in my cookbook, Sally's Baking 101.

for coating the bowl: nonstick spray, butter, or oil
1/4 cup (85g) barley malt syrup or honey

fields.description: Give snack time an upgrade with flavorful homemade artisan crackers. Crispy, salty, savory, and endlessly customizable, the simple dough comes together quickly and easily in one bowl from basic pantry ingredients.

The mixture gets a little sticky, so refer back to my tips in the blog post above.

toppings: unsweetened cocoa powder, sprinkles, crushed nuts, melted or tempered chocolate

fields.description: Using a few from-scratch components, you can create homemade eclairs, just like the kinds you enjoy from a pastry shop. These French pastries take a little time to prepare and assemble, so I encourage you to read through the recipe before beginning. You have many options for the filling, including traditional pastry cream, or any of the suggestions in the Notes or above the recipe.
ingredients: 4 large egg yolks, cold or room temperature

fields.description: The French often say, “There’s no need to leave France – we have everything here!” While it’s easy to brush it off as chauvinism, it’s true — for a country that could fit inside of Texas, there is a huge diversity of climates and terrains in one, single country. You can find everything in l’hexagone, from the windy shores of Brittany (where we’ve huddled around

fields.description:
I fried my panisses in olive oil, as is traditional, in my cast iron skillet and it's a little hard to tell you exactly how much to use but you want enough so that the panisses won't stick. This makes about 36 panisses. 

A commenter a while back noted they grilled them, which is likely possible. We can't grill in Paris as it's not authorized (I think it's because they are worried about fires), but if you want to give it a go, you can likely brush them with olive oil and cook them that way. If
ingredients: coarse salt and freshly-cracked pepper (for serving)

fields.author: David
fields.description: I’ll be at AXS design, one of my favorite shops in Paris, celebrating the release of Ready for Dessert on Sept 30th from 5:30 to 7pm. The original recipe is made with Old Tom gin, a slightly sweeter style of gin that's often amber-colored. This cocktail is very good with Old Tom gin, but if that's not something you have on hand, or want to get, the recipe works with any other type of gin, including London Dry. If you do get a bottle, you'll find yourself using it to make this drink over and over again, as I do!

Some like to add an orange or lemon twist. If you do, hold it skin side down over the glass and squeeze it to expr

1  stick (113 grams) unsalted butter,  (at room temperature)

fields.description: Like pumpkin pie, but so much easier! Made with a graham cracker and toasty pecan crust, these easy creamy pumpkin bars are irresistible.
ingredients: 1 and 1/2 cups (180g) graham cracker crumbs (about 12 full-sheet graham crackers)
1 cup (200g) light or dark brown sugar
1 and 1/2 teaspoons store-bought or homemade pumpkin pie spice*
optional for topping: whipped cream and chopped pecans

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: Sweet and salty, super easy honey pear tart with goat cheese, honey, and toasty almonds.
ingredients: 1 package (2 sheets) frozen puff pastry, thawed
egg wash: 1 large egg beaten with 1 Tablespoon milk

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: A super crunchy and flavorful pumpkin spiced granola, sweetened with maple syrup, a touch of brown sugar, and loaded with fall goodies like pecans, dried cranberries, and pumpkin seeds. Be sure to read the text of the post for substitution options. Granola is naturally gluten free if using certified GF oats.
ingredients: 3 and 3/4 cups (319g) old-fashioned whole rolled oats
2/3 cup (85g) unsweetened shredded coconut (leave out if desired)
2/3 cup (95g) pepitas (pumpkin seeds, raw or roasted)
2 large egg whites, at room temperature
1/4 cup (56g) coconut oil, melted (or vegetable oil or melted butter)
3 Tablespoons (38g) packed light or dark brown sugar
fields.title: Crunchy Pumpkin Spice Granola

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home Blog
fields.description: This farro salad features fall pears, hazelnuts and arugula, a tasty fall salad everyone will love.
ingredients: handful or two baby arugula ( or sub lacinato kale)
1 ½ ounces shaved or grated Romano Cheese ( optional, but delicious)
fields.title: Farro Salad

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | feasting at home
fields.description: A simple, delicious recipe for Fermented Hot Sauce using fresh summer chilies, with no special equipment and only 20 minutes of hands-on time!
ingredients: 6 1/4  teaspoons finely ground sea salt (or Pink Himalayan salt) – use 1 1/4 teaspoon salt, per 1 cup of water.
16 ounces fresh chili peppers, sliced in half (about 6-7 cups) seeds & stem removed, see notes
1 carrot, very thinly sliced  1/16th-inch (do not peel!)
optional: herbs (oregano, cilantro, celery leaves) and spices (cumin, coriander, chipotle powder, smoked paprika)
1-3 tablespoons apple cider vinegar, (optional- for extra tang and for more healthy probiotics)
honey or sugar to taste (optional, good if making Sriracha style)
If your hot sauce is not hot enough, you can always add cayenne or ground chipotle to taste. Free free to add spices ( 1/2- 1 teaspoon) cumin, coriander, chipotle, allspice, etc. Make this your own.
fields.title: Fermented Hot Sauce

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: Instead of pumpkin pie this season, try my pumpkin streusel bars. With a gingersnap crust and brown sugar streusel topping, everyone will want seconds.
ingredients: 1 and 1/4 cups gingersnap cookie crumbs (see note)
8 ounces (226g) full-fat brick cream cheese, softened to room temperature
1 and 1/4 cups (285g) pumpkin puree (fresh or canned)
1/3 cup (80g) plain Greek yogurt, regular yogurt, or sour cream*
1/4 teaspoon EACH ground cloves, ground nutmeg, ground ginger*
1/3 cup (70g) packed light or dark brown sugar
1/4 cup (4 Tbsp; 56g) unsalted butter, cold and cubed
optional: vanilla icing or whipped cream for topping
fields.title: Pumpkin Streusel Bars

fields.author: Tessa Arias
fields.description: Pumpkin Cookie Butter Truffles are fall perfection in each bite! Creamy pumpkin spice and Speculoos cookie filling, enrobed in rich, crisp chocolate. Easy no-bake recipe, no chocolate tempering required!
ingredients: 3 ounces (85 grams) cream cheese,  (at room temperature)
1 3/4 cup (175 grams) Speculoos cookie crumbs  ((from about 23 cookies))
Crushed Speculoos cookies or sprinkles (for garnish)

fields.description: Soft, buttery almond shortbread cookies filled with sweet raspberry jam. These easy, egg-free thumbprint cookies come together in 1 bowl and bake into tender, melt-in-your-mouth treats perfect for the holidays or any time of year. 1–2 Tablespoons (15–30g/ml) heavy cream, half-and-half, or milk

fields.description: These cake batter chocolate chip cookies are a cross between delicious, soft-baked chocolate chip cookies and sprinkle-filled funfetti cake. If you like chocolate chip cookies and you like the flavor of cake batter, you will love these soft & chewy cookies!

3/4 cup (12 Tbsp; 170g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
1 cup (180g) chocolate chips (I use 1/2 cup white and 1/2 cup semi-sweet)

fields.title: Friday Things #474

fields.description: A creamy, rich chocolate frosting is the perfect match for my super-moist chocolate chip cake. Throw in some raspberries and this cake will surely be a show-stopper!

3/4 cup (135g) mini or regular semi-sweet chocolate chips

fields.description: 35+ Best Pumpkin Recipes! Easy, delicious recipes for cookies, cakes, cheesecakes, breads, homemade pies & more. So many ideas for fall!

fields.description: Super quick and easy Raspberry Dark Chocolate Blondies are ultra ooey, gooey, and chewy blonde brownies stuffed with melty dark chocolate and tart raspberries for the perfect simple sweet treat!

3/4 cup (75 grams) fresh or frozen raspberries ((don't thaw if frozen))

Turbinado or rock sugar, for sprinkling over the rolls, or regular sugar

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at home
fields.description: Learn how to make lacto-fermented pickles! An easy method for flavorful, crunchy, tangy, garlic dill pickles with only 15 minutes of hands-on time. Full of healthy, gut-healing probiotics these little guys are perfect as a low-calorie snack, or sliced and added to sandwiches.
ingredients: 2- 2 1/2 lbs pickling cucumbers– all similar size ( 5 inches)
5 cups filtered water or tap water (that is not overly chlorinated)
2 tablespoons fine sea salt or Himalayan salt  (or basically one heaping teaspoon fine sea salt (7 grams) per one cup of water, to make a 3% brine.) * see notes.
1 teaspoon each:  fennel seeds, coriander seeds, allspice, peppercorns, dill seeds, mustard seeds, celery seeds- and feel free to add more peppercorns!
10-20 garlic cloves, sliced (or double for extra garlicky)
1-3 fresh red chilies – or dried arbol chilies, or add chili flakes (all optional)
3-4 bay leaves (or a grape leaf or oakleaf) – these are sources of tannic acid to help them stay crisp.
fields.title: Homemade Fermented Pickles with Garlic and Dill

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine
fields.description: This refreshing cherry salad recipe features lacinato kale, black rice, fresh mint, toasted hazelnuts, and creamy goat cheese in a tangy red wine vinaigrette. It is vegan-adaptable.
ingredients: 2 cups lacinato kale, packed – chopped and de-stemmed
3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, divided
1 1/2 cups cooked black rice,  (or use wild rice)
2 cups fresh cherries, pitted and cut in half
fields.title: Cherry Salad with Kale and Black Rice

fields.description: Delicious slow-roasted, Pulled Pork Tacos are rubbed with flavorful Five Spice seasoning and served with Asian Slaw and a Spicy Sriracha Mayo.  A flavorful Asian twist on pulled pork tacos!
ingredients: 1 ½ teaspoons Five-spice (use store-bought or see recipe below)
3.5-pound boneless pork shoulder (or substitute beef or lamb roast)
3 cups cabbage, shredded or finely sliced
¼ cup red onions sliced thin or 3 scallions, chopped
1/4 cup chopped cilantro, mint or Thai basil
2 tablespoons Sriracha or chili garlic sauce
Serve with warm or toasted corn/flour tortillas, pickled onions, cilantro, lime, avocado slices ( optional)
1 teaspoon fennel seed, toasted and ground
1 teaspoon Szechuan peppercorns, toasted and ground ( or sub regular peppercorns, although Szechwan is more authentic)
fields.title: Pulled Pork Tacos with Five Spice, Asian Slaw and Sriracha Aioli

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home
fields.description: A cozy, comforting, classic recipe for French Onion Soup with caramelized onions in rich deep broth topped with toasty croutons and melted gruyere.  Vegan and Vegetarian Adaptable!
ingredients: 2 tablespoons butter ( or sub olive oil)
6 cups beef stock or veggie broth (see notes)
1/4 teaspoon sherry vinegar, or red wine vinegar (or see notes)
6 ounces Gruyere, Comtéor Emmental cheese (or sub melty vegan cheese), sliced
a few sprigs fresh thyme for garnish
fields.title: French Onion Soup

A real crowd-pleaser, this rich, creamy egg filling is full of flavor and packed with bacon, caramelized onions and cheese. All baked into a crisp, deliciously buttery pie crust.
ingredients: 10 tablespoons (141 grams) unsalted butter, (cold and cubed)
1  red onion (about ½ pound or 225 grams whole), (finely sliced)
1 ¼ cups (141 grams or 1/3 pound) Gruyère, (shredded and divided)

ingredients: 3/4 cup (180ml) favorite red wine (I use a pinot noir)*
1 and 1/2 Tablespoons (8g) unsweetened natural or dutch-process cocoa powder
2 four ounce bars (226g) semi-sweet chocolate, coarsely chopped*
1/4 cup (4 Tablespoons; 56g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature

fields.description: 1. It's been a whirlwind week at our house! First up, the kids had their first day of school and they all came home so, so happy. They love their teachers, were happy to see

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home
fields.description: Stir-Fried Brussels Sprouts with Furikake. Add shrimp (or Crispy Tofu) for a quick and healthy Japanese-inspired meal that can be made in under 30 minutes- a delicious weeknight dinner! Gluten-free, Low carb, vegan-adaptable.
ingredients: 1 lb thinly sliced Brussels Sprouts ( or sub cabbage)
½ an onion (white, yellow or sweet) thinly sliced
1-2 tablespoons olive oil or coconut oil
8-10 oz large shrimp or prawns -raw or cooked, peeled and deveined (or make this Crispy Tofu)
a drizzle of toasted sesame oil
a drizzle of soy sauce (or Gluten Free Braggs)
1 tablespoon Furikake, plus more to taste!
chili flakes, chili threads, or sriracha sauce

fields.description: These salted quinoa granola bars are chewy, crunchy, and naturally sweetened with dates and honey. Made with oats, nuts, seeds, and uncooked quinoa, they’re finished with dark chocolate and flaky sea salt for the perfect balance of wholesome and indulgent. Freezer-friendly and ideal for meal prep, with plenty of substitution options listed in the recipe notes to fit your preferences.

1 Tablespoon chia seeds (feel free to skip)
2 ounces (57g) semi-sweet or bittersweet chocolate, finely chopped

fields.description: 1. Last week I mentioned that Dominic had a series of scans (and a bone marrow biopsy) and we were waiting to hear the results and next steps from his oncologist. A few days ago,

1/2 cup (100g) packed light or dark brown sugar

4 ounces shiitake mushrooms, de-stemmed, sliced (or use Smoked Mushrooms!)
1  1/2 – 2 cups cooked Japanese rice, black rice, quinoa or other grain (optional, or sub more veggies)
Feel free to add additional roasted veggies or steamed veggies

fields.description: A decadent chocolate version of a traditional trifle, and so easy to make. Layer chocolate brownies, custard, Irish cream liqueur and whipped cream for an indulgent treat

100ml Irish cream liqueur plus 1 tbsp extra for soaking the brownies

fields.description: Break through the spongey chocolate topping to reveal a puddle of sauce in this super-simple dessert with Irish cream liqueur
ingredients: 100g butter melted, plus a little extra for greasing
200ml Irish cream liqueur plus extra to serve (optional)
100g milk chocolate very roughly chopped into chunks

fields.description: 1. I just love her imagination 🩷 2. How in the world did he get to be such a big kid?! 3. Thank you for all of your beautiful words and continued prayers for Dominic

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: Rum balls are a grownup-favorite festive treat that are easy to make and fun to gift. These no-bake, boozy rum balls taste even better after a day or two in the refrigerator, so I recommend making them in advance.

2 Tablespoons (10g) natural or dutch-process unsweetened cocoa powder* (I like this dutch-process cocoa)
1 cup desiccated unsweetened coconut (100g) OR finely chopped pecans (130g) (see Note)

fields.description: Make a warming bowl of borsch (also known as borscht or borshch) with this simple and comforting recipe. Serve this hearty soup with a dollop of soured cream and a chunk of crusty bread
ingredients: 1kg fatty beef shin (left in large chunks)
200g beetroot peeled and chopped into matchsticks

100ml soured cream or crème fraîche to serve

fields.description: I share trusted baking recipes your friends will LOVE alongside insights into the science of sweets. I'm a professionally trained chef, cookbook author, and cookie queen. I love to write about all things sweet, carb-y, and homemade. I live in Phoenix, Arizona (hence the blog name!)

fields.author: Michelle Lettrich
fields.description: 1. Last Saturday was rainy and gloomy, so we had a cozy day at home and introduced the kids to Mary Poppins... they loved it! 2. Dominic completed his first neuroblastoma vaccine shot this week;

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home
fields.description: A simple homemade Garam Masala Recipe that can be made in 10 minutes - with ground fragrant spices you already have in your pantry. Easy, healthy and authentic!  See notes for toasting whole spices.

fields.author: Tessa Arias
fields.description: Learn all about sugar's role in cupcakes and see our experiments with decreasing and increasing the amount of sugar called for in a recipe.

fields.description: Use up your leftover Christmas Day vegetables to make this healthy, comforting winter soup. It's a great way to get a bit of goodness after all the festive excess

1.2l vegetable stock made from a stock cube
550g leftover roasted or boiled vegetables, such as  Brussels sprout carrots, parsnips and squash, roughly chopped
natural yogurt or crème fraîche, to serve

fields.author: Michelle Lettrich
fields.description: 1. These two have been nearly inseparable lately... I love watching their friendship grow 💙 2. Dominic had his second neuroblastoma vaccine injection this week and tolerated it well again - very painful when he

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: Gooey cinnamon-spiced and salted caramel-infused apples join my buttery flaky pie crust to make this absolutely incredible apple pie variation. I love this salted caramel apple pie with a classic lattice top, but you can try the oat streusel from this blueberry crumble pie or the crumb topping from my apple crumble pie. Bake and cool the pie completely, and then top with even more salted caramel sauce.
ingredients: Homemade Pie Crust or All Butter Pie Crust (both recipes make 2 crusts, 1 for bottom and 1 for top)
1 cup (290g) salted caramel sauce, divided (I recommend my homemade salted caramel)
10 cups (1250g) 1/4-inch-thick apple slices (about 8 large peeled and cored apples)*
1/4 teaspoon ground allspice OR ground cloves (either is fine)
egg wash: 1 large egg beaten with 1 Tablespoon (15ml) milk
optional: coarse sugar for sprinkling on crust
fields.title: Salted Caramel Apple Pie

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: Seriously addicting salty & sweet snack mix made from Nutella, peanuts, and Chex cereal. Recipe is gluten free.
ingredients: 1/2 cup (8 Tbsp; 113g) salted or unsalted butter
fields.title: Salted Peanut Nutella Puppy Chow

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine
fields.description: Linguini tossed with an entire bunch of wilted chard, garlic, lemon and toasted bread crumbs with optional chili flakes and pecorino cheese. A  healthy 30 minute vegetarian meal full of flavor!
ingredients: 4 ounces dry pasta- linguini, spaghetti, etc.
optional toppings: chili flakes, toasted pine nuts, pecorino cheese
½ cup bread- crumbled and torn

nutrition.servingSize: - with the addition of toasted bread crumbs, but no cheese.

fields.title: Garlicky Chard Pasta with Toasted Bread Crumbs

fields.author: Elena Silcock
fields.description: Vegans will love this pad Thai that you can make on Boxing Day to use up leftover brussels sprouts. You could also cook the dish using fresh sprouts
ingredients: 250g flat rice noodles (check the packet to make sure they’re vegan)
2 tsp palm sugar (or soft brown sugar)
2 spring onions thinly sliced on a diagonal
200g charred brussels sprouts left over from Christmas Day, or cook from raw
30g salted peanuts (or any other nuts you might have), roughly chopped, to serve

fields.author: Michelle Lettrich
fields.description: 1. How did this kid get so grown up?! I swear I blinked and he became a little man! 2. This girl is LOVING kindergarten! She comes home every day excited to share her papers
fields.title: Friday Things #493

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home
fields.description: A quick and easy recipe for Gazpacho, a chilled, no-cook, Spanish soup highlighting ripe and juicy summer tomatoes. Vegan and gluten-free with two variations: Chunky Gazpacho (my favorite) or Smooth Gazpacho. Allow more time for the chunky gazpacho as there is more chopping involved.
ingredients: 1 cup celery, sliced (2 celery stalks, sliced)
2 lb tomatoes (juicy ripe tomatoes: cherry tomatoes, grape tomatoes, yellow tomatoes, heirlooms or romas)
8 ounces cucumbers (3 1/2 cups) -use thin-skinned like English, Persian, Turkish, no need to peel. If using waxy cucumbers with thick skins, peel them.
2-3 teaspoons red wine vinegar or sherry vinegar
1/8 – 1/4 teaspoon piment d’espelette (or cayenne or sweet paprika),
1/2 cup fresh herbs, finely chopped ( 1/4 cup Italian parsley, 1/4 cup basil, or  sub mint, cilantro, or  dill)
optional: 1-2 slices of soaked bread (see notes)
fields.title: Best Gazpacho Recipe

fields.author: Sally
fields.description: Salted pistachio chocolate chunk slice and bake cookies have pistachio chunks, dark chocolate, brown sugar, sea salt, and a crunchy sugar edge. Their upgraded taste and texture puts them above the rest!
ingredients: 3/4 cup (12 Tbsp; 170g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
2/3 cup (133g) packed light or dark brown sugar

1 lb chicken (or sub crispy tofu or roasted cauliflower, see notes below)
1-3 tablespoons peanut oil, coconut or vegetable oil for frying – optional, see notes.
2 teaspoons fish sauce ( or vegan fish sauce, or soy sauce)
1 tablespoon soy sauce ( or liquid amino acids)
1 tablespoon rice vinegar (black vinegar if you have it)
1 tablespoon garlic chili paste (Sambal chili paste)
fields.title: Kung Pao Noodles Recipe

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine
fields.description: A simple delicious recipe for Kung Pao Zucchini that can be made in 20 minutes and served over either rice or noodles.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon olive, coconut or peanut oil
2 med-large zucchini or summer squash, cut into ½ inch thick half moons, or quarter rounds
2 teaspoons fish sauce ( or try vegan fish sauce)
1 tablespoon vinegar (black vinegar if you have it, or use rice wine vinegar)
1-3 teaspoons garlic chili paste (Sambal chili paste) or sub chili flakes to taste at the end
fields.title: Kung Pao Zucchini

fields.author: Miriam Nice
fields.description: A simple, sweet cordial you can make from leftover carrots. Don’t throw the grated carrots away after steeping - bake up a delicious carrot cake
fields.title: Carrot lemonade

fields.description: Classic mac n cheese in casserole form
fields.title: Macaroni & Cheese Casserole

fields.author: Good Food team
fields.description: Deliciously spicy - warm up with this fab veggie soup.
fields.title: Carrot, lentil & orange soup

fields.description: A delicious homemade ice cream recipe
ingredients: 2 cups malted milk balls (Whoppers, coarsely chopped)
fields.title: Malted Milk Ice Cream

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine
fields.description: Tender, garam-masala spiced Lamb Meatballs with Indian Curry Sauce melt in your mouth and are simply irresistible. A simple, flavorful dinner the whole family will love.
ingredients: 1 shallot, finely minced ( or sub ½ cup chopped onion)
1 teaspoon dried mint ( or 1 tablespoon fresh)
2 tablespoons ghee, butter or olive oil
2 tablespoons fresh ginger, grated or use paste
1 tsp fresh turmeric, grated (or ½ teaspoon ground turmeric)
2 teaspoons dried fenugreek leaves ( optional, but really good- elevates the dish )
1 extra large tomato, finely diced, with juices (about 1 cup diced tomatoes)
2 teaspoons brown sugar ( or coconut sugar or alternative )
½ teaspoon salt, more to taste
¼-½  tsp cayenne, more to taste
fields.title: Lamb Meatballs Recipe

fields.author: Cassie Best
fields.description: Take the humble carrot to new heights with an irresistible bacon and marmalade butter. This makes a great Christmas dinner side
ingredients: 800g carrots halved down the centre and sliced on an angle
fields.title: Carrots with bacon & marmalade butter

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home
fields.description: The best recipe for Lamb Shawarma- tender, juicy, leg of lamb, slow roasted in the oven with Middle Eastern Spices until falling apart. Serve this with rice and veggies, or in a pita or wrap!
ingredients: 3 lb leg of lamb, boneless (see notes for bone-in or shoulder)
one large onion, sliced into thick ½ inch slices
fields.title: Lamb Shawarma Recipe

fields.description: This malted pretzel crunch is quick and easy and the ultimate sweet/salty snack! Keep a batch of this on hand for the holidays, or package it up and give it away as gifts!
ingredients: 4 cups miniature pretzels (about ½ of a 16-ounce bag)
fields.title: Malted Pretzel Crunch

fields.author: Anjum Anand
fields.description: The full-flavoured crust gives a hint of heat to this Indian-style meal. Serve with sautéed greens for a keto-friendly dinner.
ingredients: 1 fat garlic clove finely grated until it resembles a paste
4 skinless sustainable white fish fillets, about 140g each
thumb-tip-size piece fresh root ginger roughly chopped
fields.title: Cashew, chilli & lime-crusted fish

fields.description: A fabulous maple-date jam is sandwiched between a crumb crust and topping.
ingredients: 12 ounces pitted dates (finely chopped (about 1¾ cups chopped))
½ cup unsalted butter (at room temperature)
fields.title: Maple-Date Bars

fields.author: Good Food team
fields.description: Try this delicious parsnip & maple syrup cake for your next weekend bake. Use up spare allotment veg and turn it into a tasty treat to share
ingredients: 1 medium eating apple peeled, cored and grated
fields.title: Parsnip & maple syrup cake

fields.author: Tonia | Feasting at Home
fields.description: This classic lasagna recipe is absolutly delectable! Made with a rich tomato-meat sauce, three kinds of cheeses, and savory Italian spices, it's easy to make and always a crowd-pleaser.
ingredients: 12 lasagna noodles: oven-ready or no-cook noodles (we used Rummo oven-ready lasagna noodles)– or use regular lasagna noodles and boil to al dente. ( You’ll need enough for 4 layers of noodles)
Optional garnish: sprinkle with fresh basil or Italian parsley.
½ pound ground beef  (or sub-ground turkey)
½ pound ground pork (you can use all ground beef, or ground lamb, turkey  or a meat substitute)
½ teaspoon ground fennel (or 1 teaspoon fennel seeds)
40 ounces marinara sauce (5 cups), divided. ( This is slightly less than two jars of store-bought)
1/4 cup broth (omit if using preboiled pasta noodles)- use chicken broth, vegetable broth, or beef broth. This extra liquid ensures the no-cook noodles get perfectly tender.
15 ounces ricotta cheese (or sub vegan tofu ricotta- If using the tofu ricotta, no need to add more salt or egg.) *see notes for using cottage cheese
¾  cup grated parmesan cheese or pecorino cheese, divided
2 & ½ cups grated mozzarella cheese, divided
tags: Traditional lasagna recipe with ricotta cheese
Traditional lasagna recipe with cottage cheese
fields.title: Easy Homemade Lasagna Recipe

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine| Feasting at Home
fields.description: Lebanese Chicken with 7-spice, caramelized onions, almonds, and roasted lemon. A simple flavorful Lebanese-inspired dinner, with only 15 minutes of hands-on time. See notes for grilling!
ingredients: 2 lbs chicken, skin on, bone-in thighs and legs (see notes, for boneless, skinless options )
2 1/2 teaspoons salt (use 1 teaspoon salt per pound of chicken plus an extra 1/2 teaspoon for the onions)
1 extra-large red onion, sliced into 1/2 inch wedges
1/4 cup Marcona almonds, or slivered almonds or pinenuts
fields.title: Lebanese Chicken with 7-Spice

fields.description: These sweet scones are to die for!
ingredients: 1 cup quick-cooking oats (plus additional for sprinkling)
1 pound cold unsalted butter, diced ((4 sticks ))
1  egg beaten with 1 tablespoon milk or water (for egg wash)
fields.title: Maple-Oatmeal Scones

fields.author: Ailsa Burt
fields.description: Enjoy this easy rice dish for a flavour-packed veggie main or serve alongside grilled meats, such as lamb chops or fish. It will be on the table in an hour
ingredients: 10g parsley finely chopped, plus a few whole leaves to serve
fields.title: Cauliflower baked rice

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home
fields.description: This Orzo Salad is tossed with a lemon basil dressing, with fresh cucumbers, tomatoes and arugula. A healthy, vegan, pasta salad that can be made ahead for midweek lunches or potlucks. Keep it vegan or add feta!
ingredients: 2 Turkish cucumbers, sliced (about 2 cups) or use English cucumbers
handful of baby arugula (optional or sub baby spinach)
1 cup basil ( packed down tight, about 2 ounces)
½ teaspoon salt, more to taste
fields.title: Orzo Salad Recipe

fields.author: Ruth Watson
fields.description: A lovely combination of cauliflower cheese and quiche Lorraine - make sure you pick the freshest cauli around
ingredients: 225g plain flour plus extra for rolling out
1  small  cauliflower broken into smallish florets
100g  thick-cut unsmoked streaky bacon roughly chopped
175g/6oz cheese finely grated (combine two or three of the following: parmesan, cheddar and gruyère or emmental)
fields.title: Cauliflower cheese & bacon quiche

fields.description: A pate sucree crust is filled with homemade caramel sauce and whipped milk chocolate mousse, then topped with a drizzle of caramel and milk chocolate curls.
ingredients: ½ cup unsalted butter, at room temperature, cut into 8 pieces
fields.title: Milky Way Tart

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home
fields.description: This Stuffed Eggplant recipe is so cozy and warming! Succulent eggplant is filled with basmati rice, your choice of ground meat (or lentils), herbs, and Lebanese spices and baked until meltingly tender, in a fragrant tomato broth. Vegan-adaptable.
ingredients: 3-4 medium eggplant (3 inches wide by 5 inches long)
1 lb ground lamb, ground beef, ground turkey,  ground plant-based “meat”, or 2 cups cooked lentils.
2 medium tomatoes, diced ( 1 ½ cups) plus their juices
¼ cup fresh mint ( or flat-leaf parsley )
optional: ¼ cup pinenuts, toasted (save 1 tablespoon for garnish)
2 cups broth- chicken, veggie, or use water and pinch salt

nutrition.servingSize: 1 stuffed eggplant, calculated with ground lamb

fields.title: Stuffed Eggplant Recipe

fields.author: Tonia Schemmel
fields.description: Bright and tangy, this Lemon Butter Sauce will elevate your meal! Delicious on pasta, seafood, poultry, and vegetables. Simple ingredients and easy to make in minutes!
ingredients: 2-4 tablespoons fresh herbs, minced: parsley, chives, dill, thyme, tarragon, sorrel, lovage, mint – a mix is lovely
fields.title: Lemon Butter Sauce Recipe

fields.author: Barney Desmazery
fields.description: Two great side dishes combined, this herby mashed potato with cauliflower and cheddar works perfectly with sausages, veal or chicken
ingredients: 1 large  baking potato peeled and cut into chunks
1 large head cauliflower cut into florets
good quality pork or vegetarian  sausages to serve (optional)
fields.title: Cauliflower cheese mash with thyme

fields.description: These miniature cheesecake cookies are easy and a holiday staple in our family. A vanilla wafer is topped with cheesecake batter and cherry pie filling. A perfect bite-size treat!
ingredients: 16 ounce package cream cheese, at room temperature
fields.title: Mini Cheesecake Cookies

fields.author: Good Food team
fields.description: This creamy, vegetarian soup, from Becky Lovatt, is a great way to use up leftover chestnuts, or use freshly roasted chestnuts for a more intense flavour
ingredients: ½ tbsp olive oil plus a drizzle to serve
25g grated parmesan or vegetarian alternative, to serve
fields.title: Cauliflower & chestnut soup

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home Blog
fields.description: Lemon Cloud Pie- a light and refreshing lemony dessert, that feels like a cloud!
fields.title: Gina's Lemon Cloud Pie

fields.description: A recipe for miniature cheesecakes flavored with Kahlua and espresso.
ingredients: 24 ounces PHILADELPHIA cream cheese (at room temperature)
fields.title: Mini Tiramisu Cheesecakes

fields.author: Anna Glover
fields.description: Enjoy this comforting cauliflower, cheese & leek pie. With homemade mustard and thyme pastry, it's a great veggie main course for any occasion
ingredients: 500g plain flour plus extra for dusting
fields.title: Cauliflower, leek & cheddar pie with mustard & thyme pastry

fields.description: The famous Compost Cookie recipe from Momofuku Milk Bar.
ingredients: 1 cup unsalted butter, at room temperature
fields.title: Compost Cookies

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home
fields.description: This easy Lemon Glaze is made with three simple ingredients in under 3 minutes flat! It hardens, stays white, and holds its shape, perfect for cakes, scones, cookies and morning breads. Vegan & gluten-free.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons lemon juice, more to taste or thin.
fields.title: Lemon Glaze Recipe

fields.author: Das Kochrezept
fields.description: A great veggie dish to serve up when you need a bit of comfort midweek
ingredients: 600g waxy potatoes such as Maris Peer
1 large cauliflower (about 1.2kg) cut into bite size florets
1 tbsp unsalted butter plus extra for the baking dish
fields.title: Cauliflower & potato bake

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home
fields.description: This bright and zesty Lemon Pasta recipe is studded with garlic and shallots, lemon zest, red pepper flakes, and optional pecorino cheese. Easy to make in under 25 minutes. Vegan and gluten-free adaptable.
ingredients: 8 ounces pasta- spaghetti or linguine (substitute gluten-free pasta)
1 medium lemon- zest and lemon juice to taste.
¼-½ teaspoon red pepper flakes (or use Aleppo chili flakes)
Salt and black pepper to taste
optional- ¼ cup fresh grated pecorino cheese (or parmesan cheese)
Fresh herbs- 1/4 cup Italian parsley, chopped, or sub fresh basil or dill
fields.title: Lemon Garlic Pasta Recipe

fields.description: All the fabulous flavor of Muddy Buddy snacks with the addition of ooey, gooey marshmallow.
fields.title: Muddy Buddy Cereal Treats

fields.description: My Dad's favorite birthday cake!
ingredients: 16 ounces cream cheese, at room temperature
fields.title: Chocolate Cake with Cream Cheese Filling & Walnut-Sugar Topping

fields.author: Cassie Best
fields.description: Make some retro pastry cases add your favourite fillings – choose from coronation chicken, prawn cocktail, potato curry, and pea and mint
fields.title: Celebration vol-au-vents

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home
fields.description: This lemony chickpea soup is so warming and flavorful! The broth is infused with fennel bulb and sumac, then drizzled with olive oil, and sprinkled with fresh herbs. Vegan and Gluten-free.
ingredients: 1 cup dry chickpeas, soaked 8-24 hours (or sub 2 14-ounce cans, drained)
Pinch of  chili flakes, aleppo or Urfa Biber (Turkish Chili Flakes)
Garnish: fresh dill or flat-leaf parsley ( or both- even better), olive oil or zhoug sauce.
fields.title: Brothy Chickpea Soup Recipe

fields.author: Good Food team
fields.description: A new take on champ using Italian kale-like cabbage, bacon and celeriac, a versatile root that's lighter than potato
ingredients: 1 ½kg celeriac peeled and cut into chunks
250g cavolo nero or green cabbage, finely shredded
fields.title: Celeriac, cavolo nero & bacon mash

fields.description: An authentic recipe for Grilled Lemongrass Chicken- bursting with Vietnamese flavor! Simple, Easy and delicious!
ingredients: 2- 2 1/4 lbs chicken thighs, boneless and skinless
1 tablespoon soy sauce ( or GF Bragg’s Amino Acid)
1-3 teaspoons Chili Garlic Sauce ( optional, for heat)
fields.title: Grilled Lemongrass Chicken

fields.description: Slab pie with a tender pastry crust and filled with both yellow and white nectarines. A summer must!
ingredients: 1½ cups unsalted butter, cold, cut into small pieces
1 pound white nectarines (about 4 medium, pitted and cut into ¼-inch slices)
1 pound yellow nectarines (about 4 medium, pitted and cut into ¼-inch slices)
½ cup almond meal or oat flour
fields.title: Nectarine and Honey Slab Pie

fields.author: Good Food team
fields.description: Give oven chips a new twist - with a helping of celeriac
fields.title: Celeriac oven chips

fields.description: This easy no-bake s'mores pie is made with distinct layers of a graham cracker crust, thick chocolate filling, and a toasted marshmallow topping!
fields.title: S'mores Pie

fields.author: Good Food team
fields.description: This warming and creamy dish is perfect for sharing with friends and family
ingredients: 1 celeriac (about 500g) peeled, quartered and thinly sliced
500g potatoes peeled and very thinly sliced
fields.title: Celeriac, potato & rosemary gratin

fields.author: Tonia | Feasting at Home
fields.description: This recipe for Mushroom Lentil Stew with Fennel and Sage is earthy, hearty and richly spiced.  So satisfying and vegan!
ingredients: 2 yellow onions, about 2 cups chopped small
1/4 teaspoon red pepper flakes (optional-adjust to taste)
1 teaspoon ground anise seeds (or use ground fennel)
1 lb cremini mushrooms (or a mix with shiitake, oysters, chanterelles, lions mane etc.)

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine
fields.description: Fast, easy recipe for Lo Mein that can be made in under 20 minutes. Loaded up with healthy vegetables, this easy dinner is perfect for busy weeknights! Keep it vegan or add chicken breast or shrimp. Gluten-free adaptable.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons Chinese cooking wine (or mirin, or sub 1 1/2 tablespoons water mixed with 1/2 teaspoon rice vinegar and 1/2 teaspoon honey)
1 teaspoon maple syrup, sugar or honey
2 tablespoons wok oil, peanut oil or coconut oil
Other options: a handful of baby spinach, bok choy, shredded veggies like brussel sprouts, bean sprouts. In spring, asparagus is nice, in winter, roasted veggies work well too!
fields.title: Lo Mein Noodle

fields.description: Soft and chewy Nutella cookies baked in crinkle cookie style with extra chopped hazelnuts.
fields.title: Nutella Cookies

fields.author: Tom Kerridge
fields.description: A simple recipe for an old favourite, using raw celeriac, mayonnaise, mustard, chives, celery salt and a hint of lemon
ingredients: 1 tbsp celery salt plus extra for seasoning
fields.title: Celeriac remoulade

fields.author: Sylvia Fountaine | Feasting at Home
fields.description: This Mediterranean-inspired lentil salad recipe is bursting with flavor! Cold and refreshing, it's made with simple ingredients in just 30 mins!
ingredients: 1 cup uncooked lentils- French lentils, black lentils, brown lentils (2 ½ cups cooked)
1 pint cherry tomatoes- or grape tomatoes, sliced in half, about 2 cups
1 English cucumber- or Turkish cucumber, diced, about 2 cups
1 bell pepper- yellow or red, diced
¼ cup red onion, very finely diced
¼- ½ cup fresh mint, chopped
2 garlic cloves, grated or finely minced
¼ cup extra virgin olive oil
